description: 40-slide application deck for SettleMint participation in the PVARA framework

slides.title: SettleMint Application to PVARA
slides.subtitle: Digital asset lifecycle infrastructure for regulated virtual asset operations | April 2026

slides.title: Purpose of this application
slides.body:
• SettleMint seeks to participate in Pakistan's virtual asset framework in a regulated, transparent, and institution-led way.
• The company is applying on the basis that PVARA is building a supervised market with strong AML, governance, and consumer protection expectations.
• SettleMint's role is not speculative retail activity. The role is regulated digital asset infrastructure, controlled issuance, operational governance, and lifecycle management.
• SettleMint welcomes PVARA guidance on the correct final category for its intended operating model.

slides.title: What SettleMint brings to the framework
slides.item1:
Institutional operating model
Built for banks, market infrastructures, and sovereign programmes rather than unmanaged retail flows.
slides.item2:
Lifecycle coverage
Design, issuance, transfer control, servicing, redemption, and reporting on one platform.
slides.item3:
Governance and controls
Role-based access, maker-checker approvals, audit logs, and identity-linked actions.
slides.item4:
Regulatory alignment
Configurable compliance, jurisdiction controls, custody integration, and reporting readiness.

slides.title: A regulated market needs regulated infrastructure
slides.body: Pakistan is building a supervised virtual asset market. That creates an opportunity to start with the right operating model: identity-linked onboarding, controlled issuance, governed transfers, clear auditability, and integration with regulated institutions. SettleMint is built for exactly that environment.

slides.title: SettleMint at a glance
slides.col1:
Company focus
• Digital asset lifecycle infrastructure
• Regulated institutions and sovereign programmes
• Platform delivery, not speculative activity
slides.col2:
Core product
• DALP, Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform
• Governance, compliance, servicing, and integration
• Multi-asset support on EVM-compatible networks
slides.col3:
Market relevance
• Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific exposure
• Strong fit for regulator-led digital asset markets
• Built for operational accountability

slides.title: What SettleMint is and is not
slides.col1:
What SettleMint is
• A platform company for digital asset lifecycle operations
• An infrastructure provider for regulated tokenized products
• A partner to institutions that need governance and controls
slides.col2:
What SettleMint is not
• Not an unregulated exchange
• Not a custody provider by default
• Not a speculative crypto trading operator
• Not a shortcut around regulatory supervision

slides.title: Why this matters to PVARA
slides.body:
• PVARA is building a framework centered on supervision, AML compliance, market integrity, and consumer protection.
• Infrastructure choices made early in the market shape how safely and efficiently the ecosystem scales.
• SettleMint gives regulated participants a way to launch governed digital asset products without rebuilding controls from scratch.
• That reduces execution risk for both operators and regulators.

slides.title: DALP Platform
slides.subtitle: The operating layer behind compliant digital assets

slides.title: What DALP does
slides.body:
• DALP is SettleMint's Digital Asset Lifecycle Platform for designing, issuing, governing, servicing, and monitoring digital assets.
• It covers the full lifecycle rather than a single issuance event.
• It connects identity, compliance, approval workflows, token operations, and reporting in one operating model.
• It is designed for regulated environments where auditability and operational discipline matter.

slides.title: The lifecycle covered by DALP
slides.col1:
Design and configure
• Asset setup
• Metadata schemas
• Policy templates
• Governance roles
slides.col2:
Issue and distribute
• Controlled minting
• Eligibility checks
• Identity-linked onboarding
• Transfer restrictions
slides.col3:
Operate and service
• Distributions
• Redemptions
• Monitoring and logs
• Ongoing reporting outputs

slides.title: Operational control plane
slides.body: DALP provides a single control plane for operations teams. Users can configure assets, review compliance settings, track activity, and monitor live operations without losing control of governance or auditability.

slides.title: Six DALP capabilities most relevant to PVARA
slides.item1:
Identity-linked eligibility
Control who may subscribe, hold, and transfer regulated assets.
slides.item2:
Configurable compliance
Apply policy rules without rebuilding the entire product each time.
slides.item3:
Governed operations
Segregate configuration, approval, and execution responsibilities.
slides.item4:
Auditability
Track operational actions and token events with structured logs.
slides.item5:
Lifecycle servicing
Run payouts, redemptions, and maturity events in a controlled way.
slides.item6:
Enterprise integration
Connect to custody, compliance, payments, and reporting systems.

slides.title: Regulatory Fit
slides.subtitle: Alignment to the published PVARA direction

slides.title: Fit with PVARA's public priorities
slides.item1:
Licensing and supervision
SettleMint's model is built to operate inside a supervised perimeter, not outside it.
slides.item2:
AML and CFT readiness
Identity-linked controls, logs, governance, and process integration support accountable operations.
slides.item3:
Consumer protection
Permissioned operations, transfer restrictions, disclosures, and operating controls help reduce unmanaged risk.
slides.item4:
Market integrity
Pre-execution rule enforcement and auditable activity support disciplined market operations.

slides.title: Current application assumption
slides.col1:
Published process understood so far
• NOC application
• FMU goAML registration
• Local incorporation
• Final VASP licensing step
slides.col2:
SettleMint position
• Prepared to follow the process
• Needs final confirmation of the correct participation category
• Intends to work transparently with PVARA on scope and structure

slides.title: Best-effort requirement mapping
slides.body: This mapping is based on the currently published PVARA process and public summaries reviewed during drafting. It shows where SettleMint is already aligned, where the platform supports compliance readiness, and where the final position depends on PVARA confirming the correct participation category for the Pakistan operating model.
rows: Supported through controls and process model
Supported with operating model definition required

slides.title: Governance controls
slides.item1:
Role-based access
Separate operator, approver, admin, and oversight responsibilities.
slides.item2:
Maker-checker workflows
High-risk actions require formal approval steps.
slides.item3:
Identity-linked actions
Operational permissions are tied to verified users and roles.
slides.item4:
Transfer controls
Restrict movement by jurisdiction, investor type, or approval state.
slides.item5:
Audit trails
Capture who did what, when, and under which policy.
slides.item6:
Change discipline
Update policy configurations through governed workflows rather than ad hoc actions.

slides.title: Identity and verification
slides.body: Identity is a control layer, not an afterthought. DALP links verified identities and claims to eligibility decisions so that subscription and transfer actions are based on who the participant is, not just which wallet they use.

slides.title: Policy template management
slides.body: Reusable policy templates allow compliance teams to create reviewable controls once, then apply them consistently across multiple instruments and programmes. That helps regulated operators scale without losing discipline.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/jpg/Policy Templates - List.jpg

slides.title: Why lifecycle control matters
slides.body:
• Many tokenization stacks focus on issuance only. Regulated assets need more than that.
• They need transfers controlled over time, eligibility updated when rules change, servicing events executed correctly, and audit trails maintained throughout the product lifecycle.
• DALP is designed around that full operating reality.
• That makes it better suited to supervised market development than point tools built for one-off launches.

slides.title: Use Case Scope
slides.subtitle: What SettleMint can support in a regulated Pakistan market

slides.title: Use cases suited to DALP
slides.item1:
Tokenized investment products
Controlled issuance and governed investor participation.
slides.item2:
Real-world asset structures
Digital representations with lifecycle servicing and permissions.
slides.item3:
Fund and note structures
Governed subscriptions, distributions, and reporting outputs.
slides.item4:
Permissioned marketplace models
Eligibility-based participation with approval and transfer controls.
slides.item5:
Institution-led digital asset programmes
Infrastructure for banks, issuers, and licensed intermediaries.
slides.item6:
Future market expansion
Re-use the same platform and controls across additional instruments.

slides.title: What requires classification and approval
slides.col1:
Areas where SettleMint is strong
• Platform infrastructure
• Controlled issuance models
• Governance and compliance architecture
• Lifecycle servicing and reporting
slides.col2:
Areas that depend on PVARA classification
• Exact license category
• Whether the Pakistan role is issuer, investment platform, or another class
• Whether a separate local operating entity is required for specific activities

slides.title: Built for controlled participation, not unmanaged distribution
slides.body: SettleMint's strongest fit is where regulators and institutions want accountability from the start: known participants, clear onboarding, permissioned flows, and traceable operations. That is the right foundation for a new framework.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/jpg/Real Estate Assets - Listing.jpg

slides.title: Security and Operations
slides.subtitle: Operational readiness for a supervised environment

slides.title: Security and operations model
slides.item1:
Access control
Role-based permissions, approval segregation, and accountable operator actions across configuration, issuance, and servicing workflows.
slides.item2:
Custody connectivity
Integration patterns with institutional custody providers where required, so asset operations can align to approved safeguarding models.
slides.item3:
Operational observability
Logs, monitoring, event traceability, and reviewable operational records across platform actions, policy changes, and lifecycle events.
slides.item4:
Deployment flexibility
Support for dedicated and controlled deployment models aligned to regulatory, data residency, and operating risk requirements.

slides.title: Consumer protection by design
slides.col1:
Protection mechanisms
• Eligibility checks before execution
• Controlled permissions
• Structured product rules
• Clear operational accountability
slides.col2:
Why that matters
• Reduces unmanaged participant risk
• Improves supervisory visibility
• Supports transparent market conduct
• Creates a safer operating environment for new products

slides.title: Proposed market entry path
slides.col1:
Phase 1
• Confirm category with PVARA
• Submit NOC application
• Align business plan and documents
• Validate the initial scope and regulatory perimeter
slides.col2:
Phase 2
• Complete AML and local readiness steps
• Set up the approved Pakistan structure
• Define governance and reporting model
slides.col3:
Phase 3
• Launch a tightly governed first use case
• Operate with controlled participants
• Expand only after the initial model is proven

slides.title: Recommended starting point
slides.body:
• Start with a permissioned and well-defined operating model.
• Focus on one category where governance, onboarding, and lifecycle servicing can be demonstrated clearly.
• Avoid broad retail complexity in the first phase.
• Build a regulator-visible reference model that proves controls before scaling further.

slides.title: Why SettleMint is relevant at this stage
slides.item1:
Early framework stage
Good infrastructure choices early reduce rework later, especially when governance, onboarding, and lifecycle controls are built into the market from day one.
slides.item2:
Institution-led growth
Pakistan's framework can be shaped around disciplined market participation.
slides.item3:
Broad product potential
The market can evolve beyond exchange-only activity into tokenized financial products and RWA structures.
slides.item4:
Scalable operating model
The same platform and controls can support multiple approved use cases over time.

slides.title: Asset design and policy configuration
slides.body: DALP allows teams to configure instruments and associated policy controls through a structured operational interface. This helps regulated operators move faster without giving up governance discipline.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/jpg/Asset Designer - Step 6 - Compliance Modules.jpg

slides.title: Requested Outcome
slides.subtitle: What SettleMint is asking from PVARA

slides.title: Requested next steps with PVARA
slides.col1:
SettleMint requests
• Consideration for the next stage of engagement
• Confirmation of the correct participation category
• Guidance on any additional documents required
slides.col2:
SettleMint commits
• To work inside the approved regulatory perimeter
• To align the operating model to AML, governance, and consumer protection expectations
• To proceed transparently and in coordination with the Authority

slides.title: Closing statement
slides.body:
• SettleMint believes Pakistan has the opportunity to build a properly governed virtual asset market from the start.
• The company can contribute regulated-grade infrastructure for issuance, governance, lifecycle operations, and accountable market participation.
• The goal is not speed at the expense of control. The goal is a defensible market foundation.
• SettleMint welcomes the opportunity to continue the process with PVARA.
